hero.name: Divyansh Singh
hero.tagline: Backend Developer • AI Automation • Java / Spring Boot / Next.js

about.blurb: Backend-focused engineer with experience building scalable, production-grade systems in Java, Spring Boot, and SQL. I built NexFlow — a production AI workflow automation platform with a custom parallel execution engine, real-time WebSocket canvas, and multi-LLM integration (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, Mistral). Hands-on experience with Agentic AI flows, MCP (Model Context Protocol), and RAG architectures. Strong in API design, distributed messaging, cloud deployment, and writing systems that can be owned by a team — not just shipped once.

points: Engineered backend components for a telecom platform using Java (11+), Spring Boot, and RDBMS to improve performance and scalability in an Agile/Scrum setup.
Designed and implemented RESTful APIs for user registration, billing, and service provisioning, reducing response times.
Integrated CI/CD pipelines (Maven, Git, Docker) to automate deployments and accelerate release cycles.
Maintained code quality through JUnit/Mockito tests, peer reviews, and adherence to SOLID and SDLC best practices.
Applied functional programming patterns in Java (streams, immutability, declarative logic) to express complex business rules concisely.
Built a production-grade no-code AI workflow automation platform from scratch — canvas-based flow builder, real-time execution engine, and full observability into every run.
Engineered a custom parallel execution engine (Fork / Join) using CompletableFuture with WAIT_ALL, WAIT_FIRST, and WAIT_N quorum strategies for true concurrent branch execution.
Integrated 5 LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, Mistral) through a unified AI node with structured JSON output, schema enforcement, and automatic retry on parse failure.
Built real-time execution canvas over WebSocket (SockJS / STOMP) — every node lights up live, with per-node status, duration, and full I/O snapshots persisted to PostgreSQL.
Implemented Agentic flow architecture: flows can call sub-flows synchronously or asynchronously, enabling composable automation primitives and reusable agent chains.
Designed the nex.* context propagation layer — every node output is named and referenced downstream by path (nex.fetchUser.body.email), eliminating opaque UUIDs.
Built full auth stack: JWT + Spring Security + Google OAuth2 with HttpOnly cookies, OTP email verification via SMTP, and role-based access (ADMIN, SUB_ADMIN, USER).
Deployed on AWS EC2 (t3.micro) with Docker Compose, nginx reverse proxy, Cloudflare tunnel, and swap memory — Next.js 14 frontend on Vercel with full SEO, OG images, and JSON-LD.
Built a microservices-based edtech platform with 5+ Spring Boot services, integrating PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Redis for high availability and intelligent inter-service communication.
Secured workflows with JWT, Spring Security, and an API Gateway for fine-grained authN/authZ.
Improved scalability with Redis caching, async messaging (RabbitMQ), and optimized DB queries to keep the system reliable under heavy load.
Embedded a self-service mindset: structured logging, metrics, and monitoring for proactive troubleshooting instead of reactive firefighting.
Used containerization (Docker) and CI/CD automation for smooth deployments.
Built a responsive campus platform using Django, HTML, JavaScript, and Tailwind CSS to improve college-wide digital access.
Delivered reusable modules (Placement Cell, Note Sharing) like low-code blocks so non-tech users could extend features without heavy dev effort.
Added alumni–student interaction and placement tracking to increase employability visibility.
Led a 4-person team through full SDLC, achieving ~95% user satisfaction.
Worked with threads / memory / caching considerations to ensure backend scalability.
